---
title: "Recipe 13: Sexual System and Color Complexity"
format: html
---

**My images are TP**

```{r}
library(here)
# ── Recipe 13: Sexual System and Color Complexity ────────────────
source("https://michaelalfaro.github.io/eeb187-demos/recipes/code/helpers.R")   # loads ape, phytools, nlme, ggplot2, theme_poster

# ── 1. Load your pavo metrics and tree ──────────────────────────
traits <- read.csv("data/",
                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
tree   <- ape::read.tree("path/to/your-family-mini-tree.tre")

# ── 2. Pull sexual system data from FishBase ─────────────────────
# rfishbase 5.x uses `ReproMode` (older versions used `Mode`).
fb <- pull_fishbase(traits$species,
                    fields = list(reproduction = c("ReproMode")))

cat("Raw ReproMode values from FishBase:\n")
print(table(fb$ReproMode, useNA = "always"))

# ── 3. Merge and clean to binary predictor ──────────────────────
merged <- merge(traits, fb, by = "species")

merged$sexual_system <- ifelse(
  grepl("protogyn", merged$ReproMode, ignore.case = TRUE), "protogynous",
  ifelse(grepl("dioec|gonochor", merged$ReproMode, ignore.case = TRUE),
         "gonochoristic", NA))

cat("\nSpecies retained by sexual system:\n")
print(table(merged$sexual_system, useNA = "always"))

# Drop species with NA sexual system (protandrous, simultaneous, missing)
n_before <- nrow(merged)
merged   <- merged[!is.na(merged$sexual_system), ]
cat("\nDropped", n_before - nrow(merged), "species with NA or other sexual system\n")
cat("Final N:", nrow(merged), "\n")

# ── 4. Match tree to filtered data ──────────────────────────────
matched <- match_tree_data(tree, merged, species_col = "species")
cat("Species in PGLS:", nrow(matched$data), "\n")
cat("  Protogynous:", sum(matched$data$sexual_system == "protogynous"), "\n")
cat("  Gonochoristic:", sum(matched$data$sexual_system == "gonochoristic"), "\n")

# ── 5. PGLS: pattern complexity ~ sexual system ──────────────────
model <- safe_pgls(m ~ sexual_system, df = matched$data, tree = matched$tree)
print(summary(model))

# ── 6. Boxplot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(matched$data,
       aes(x = sexual_system, y = m, fill = sexual_system)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 3, alpha = 0.6, color = "grey30") +
  labs(
    title = "Pattern complexity by sexual system",
    x     = "",
    y     = "Transition density (m)"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("gonochoristic" = "#264653",
                                "protogynous"   = "#e9c46a")) +
  theme_poster +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave("results/recipe13-sexual-system-boxplot.pdf",
       width = 6, height = 5)
cat("Saved: results/recipe13-sexual-system-boxplot.pdf\n")

# ── 7. Optional: repeat for other pavo variables ─────────────────
# You can swap m for A, Jc, Jt, m_dS, or m_dL depending on
# whether you have a specific hypothesis about hue vs. pattern.
# e.g.: safe_pgls(Jc ~ sexual_system, df = matched$data, tree = matched$tree)
```
